title: Junior.Crypt.2026 CTF
description:
Junior.Crypt.2026 CTF is a 24-hour online cybersecurity competition for students and anyone eager to test their skills and have fun.

The event is organized by Beavers0 CTF team. 

Join solo or form a team of up to three members and take on challenges in Crypto, Forensics, Misc, OSINT, PWN, Reverse, and Web.

title: BroncoCTF 2026
description:
CTF Discord: https://discord.gg/WT7HXqx8jP

BroncoCTF is an international CTF competition hosted by BroncoSec. It ran in-person conjunction with Hack for Humanity, Santa Clara University's social good hackathon.

This year, we are sponsored by SANS Institute, who sponsored the course scholarship prize for our in-person event!

In BroncoCTF, teams solve various cybersecurity challenges by hunting for secret flags hidden in programs, cryptosystems, files, and even games! Use your ethical hacking skills to solve digital puzzles involving real-world technologies.

BroncoCTF 2026 will be our fifth CTF, and our third year hosting the event globally!

This event was run in-person and with limited online participation in February. However, now that we have worked out most (all?) of the issues, we are running it again, this time to a larger audience.

There is no maximum team size.

title: EYCC CTF 2026
description:
EYCC (Egyptian Youth Cybersecurity Challenge) is Egypt's national CTF 
competition for high school students, now in its second edition.

Format: Hybrid — 48h Online Qualifiers → On-site Grand Finals
Eligibility: High school students in Egypt only
Team Size: 2–5 members
Entry: Free

Categories: Web, Crypto, Forensics, Reverse Engineering, OSINT, Pwn, Misc

Website: https://eycc.stemeghackclub.org/
Discord: https://discord.gg/RZZC8SwHTt
